public_goals: Reach a workable parenting discussion without reliving the whole marriage every time.
Be taken seriously about the emotional impact of the conflict.
Keep the current parenting issue from collapsing into another unproductive spiral.
private_concerns: Feels chronically unseen unless they retell the history in full.
Worries that slowing down means their pain will be minimized again.
Is embarrassed by how quickly the conversation becomes emotionally overwhelming.
red_lines: Will not accept being told to simply calm down without their underlying pain being acknowledged.
Will not continue a process that feels emotionally out of control and directionless.
emotional_triggers: Feeling that earlier betrayal or abandonment is being minimized.
Being asked to skip directly to solutions when still emotionally activated.
Hearing the other parent describe the past as irrelevant.

disclosure_tendencies.baseline: shares emotional meaning more readily than logistical detail
disclosure_tendencies.under_stress: returns to loaded relationship history and loses issue focus
disclosure_tendencies.with_perceived_bias: floods faster and becomes less able to narrow the discussion

response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure.if_ai_pushes_resolution_too_fast: returns to narrative recurrence and loses focus

interest_profile: Not being retraumatized by the mediation format
starting_positions: Primary position: the process keeps reopening emotional injury and cannot keep going this way.
Secondary position: a human mediator may be needed if the conversation cannot stabilize.
likely_openings: Every time we try to discuss this, it turns into the same emotional collapse.
I still want resolution, but not if this keeps spiraling the same way.
